feature_name: AEP Library — Custom Adversary Emulation Pathway Builder
goal: Let org admins create, configure, and test custom Adversary Emulation Pathways within the Red Teaming workspace, so security teams can run social engineering simulations with scenarios tailored to their organization.
user_segments: Org admin (primary — creates and manages custom AEPs)
Campaign manager (secondary — selects AEPs when building campaigns, no edit access)
Red team operator (secondary — reviews AEP behavior via conversation outcomes)
success_metrics: Org admin can create a custom AEP without engineering support
Custom AEP can be validated via live simulator before campaign deployment
Custom AEP is available for selection in the campaign builder immediately after save
AEP Library is non-empty at feature launch (Dune-seeded AEPs ship simultaneously)
constraints: RBAC: only org admins can create or edit custom AEPs
Custom AEP limit per tenant: 4–5 (exact number TBD)
Dune ships 3–4 seeded AEPs that are read-only for all tenants
Lives in: Agentic Simulation > Red Teaming > AEP Library
AEP behavior is LLM-driven — branching is emergent, not scripted
Builder UX approach not yet decided — design recommendation required
assumptions: Custom AEP creation is prompt-based — admin writes a natural language instruction
A live chat simulator is part of the creation/editing flow for testing behavior
Dune-seeded AEPs are read-only and cannot be edited by any tenant user
Custom AEPs can be used in campaigns immediately after creation (no approval gate — unconfirmed)
Adversary Method is a controlled taxonomy, not free-form text
gaps: Relationship between free-text prompt and structured sections (Workflow Steps, Branching Logic) is undefined — generated vs. manually authored
Whether Adversary Method is a fixed taxonomy or free-form label is unconfirmed
Outcome criteria authoring model is unclear — per-AEP custom, Dune-global, or generated from prompt
Live simulator scope undefined — admin plays target, or AI-vs-AI test loop
Recovery path when custom AEP limit is reached is not defined
Versioning policy for AEPs used in active campaigns is not stated
Duplication from Dune-seeded AEPs as templates is not confirmed
Review/approval gate before custom AEP becomes campaign-eligible is not defined
Character/token limit on the AEP system prompt is not specified
edge_cases: Custom AEP limit reached — create button state and recovery path
Deleting a custom AEP referenced in an active campaign
Editing a custom AEP mid-campaign — behavior change risk for in-flight conversations
AEP name collision between two custom AEPs
Simulator unavailable or timing out — loading and error states
Admin without prompt engineering experience creates a nonsensical or unsafe AEP
First-time empty state: tenant has created zero custom AEPs
Campaign manager views the AEP Library (read-only, no edit/create affordance)
Very long prompt approaching token limit — counter and guard
AEP used in a completed (historical) campaign is deleted — reference integrity

critical_questions.question: Is AEP behavior defined by a single system prompt, or are Workflow Steps and Branching Logic independently authored fields that feed the LLM separately?
Is Adversary Method a fixed taxonomy with a known list, or a free-form label? Does the selection affect LLM behavior?
Are outcome criteria (Complicit, Non Complicit, Undetermined, No Response) defined globally by Dune or authored per AEP in the builder?
In the live simulator, does the admin play the target role manually, or does a second AI model simulate employee responses?
When the custom AEP limit is reached, what is the intended recovery path — delete, request increase, or upgrade?
If a custom AEP is edited after being referenced in an active campaign, does the campaign use the locked version at creation time or the live edited version?
Can an org admin duplicate a Dune-seeded AEP as a starting point for a custom one?
Is there a review or approval step before a custom AEP becomes available in the campaign builder?
Is there a character or token limit on the AEP system prompt field?

design_risks: Prompt quality variance — free-text prompt gives admins without prompt engineering experience the ability to create unreliable or unsafe AEPs; simulator is the only quality gate but it's subjective
No automated validation — a bad AEP prompt may behave well in simulator and fail unpredictably in production, triggering HR-sensitive conversations
Editing mid-campaign creates data integrity risk — behavior changes for in-flight conversations may invalidate outcome classification
Custom limit (4–5) is very low — admins may be reluctant to experiment if the limit feels permanent; drafts vs. published distinction would help
Empty library blocks the whole feature — if Dune-seeded AEPs don't ship simultaneously, the library launches unusable
teaching_notes: AEPs are live LLM system prompts, not decision trees — branching is emergent model behavior, not a wired node graph; avoid flowchart or node editor metaphors in the builder UI
Outcome classification (Complicit/Non Complicit) feeds directly into employee risk scoring — outcome criteria fields are high-stakes data inputs, not display labels
Adversary Method taxonomy likely maps to influence science categories (Authority, Urgency, Scarcity, Reciprocity, Social Proof, Liking) used across the platform for vulnerability reporting
Closest Dune precedent for prompt editor: remediation agent instruction field — use Stillsuit DS v2 TextArea or CodeEditor component as the design baseline
Library must visually distinguish Dune-seeded (read-only) from Custom (editable) AEPs — not just a label, but distinct card affordance
